question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted EVM address in the :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter that resolves via EntityService.getEncodedId() to a different account's encoded ID, causing the staking_reward_transfer table to be queried for that victim account and leaking its full reward history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a base32 alias that matches a deleted account (where coalesce(deleted, false) <> true is false) to bypass EntityService.isValidAccount() and retrieve staking reward records that should be inaccessible for a deleted entity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the two-step check (getEncodedId then isValidAccount) race window where the account is deleted between the two async calls, causing the controller to serve reward data for an account that no longer exists and whose pending_reward should be considered forfeited?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple timestamp= query parameters with eq operators to force updateConditionsAndParamsWithInValues() to build an IN-list condition that bypasses the intended single-timestamp filter, enumerating all reward payouts across arbitrary time windows and reconstructing the full reward accumulation history of any account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user set timestamp=gte:0 to retrieve all staking reward transfers from the beginning of time for any account, leaking the complete reward history including amounts that reveal the account's staking behavior and balance trajectory?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user set limit=1 and repeatedly follow the links.next pagination cursor (built from lastRow.timestamp) to enumerate every single staking reward transfer for any account without restriction, effectively harvesting the full reward history one record at a time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the order=asc query parameter to reverse the pagination direction and use the links.next cursor to traverse reward records in ascending order, bypassing any implicit assumption that DESC ordering limits exposure of historical reward data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a numeric account ID (e.g., 0.0.800, the staking reward account itself) to listStakingRewardsByAccountId() and retrieve all reward transfers paid out from that system account, leaking the global reward distribution schedule and amounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user query /accounts/0.0.800/rewards and observe that the staking reward account (account 800) has no outbound reward records, then use the absence of data to infer the current unpaid pending_reward pool size and time future reward claims?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a timestamp value of 0 (epoch zero) to retrieve reward records with consensus_timestamp = 0, potentially matching genesis-era or improperly initialized records that expose reward amounts that were never intended to be publicly queryable?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EVM address that collides with a contract account's address to resolve via getEncodedId() to a contract entity, then retrieve staking reward transfers for that contract (which may have accumulated large pending_reward values) without any ownership check?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user query the rewards endpoint for any account ID they do not own, since there is no authentication or authorization check in listStakingRewardsByAccountId(), and use the returned amount fields to determine the exact pending_reward balance and plan a front-running attack on reward claims?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isValidAccount() only checks entity existence (not entity type) to query reward records for a topic or token entity ID, causing StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to return unexpected rows if the staking_reward_transfer table contains cross-type entries?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply timestamp=lte:<future_timestamp> to retrieve reward records that have been pre-inserted for future consensus timestamps, leaking scheduled or projected reward payouts before they are officially distributed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user set limit to the maximum allowed value and combine it with timestamp=gte:0 to retrieve the maximum number of reward records in a single request, enabling bulk harvesting of reward history for high-value staking accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on the /accounts/:id/rewards endpoint to issue thousands of requests enumerating all account IDs sequentially, mapping which accounts have received staking rewards and their amounts to identify high-value targets for social engineering or phishing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply timestamp=eq:<specific_consensus_timestamp> to pinpoint the exact moment a staking reward was paid to a target account, correlating this with on-chain transaction data to determine the account's staking node and balance at that time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination link generation in getPaginationLink() by manipulating the lastRow.timestamp value through a crafted timestamp filter, causing the next link to point to reward records of a different account?
